title: Ranking Manipulation for Conversational Search Engines
authors: Samuel Pfrommer, Yatong Bai, Tanmay Gautam, Somayeh Sojoudi (UC Berkeley)
abstract: Major search engine providers are rapidly incorporating LLM-generated content in response to user queries. These conversational search engines operate by loading retrieved website text into the LLM context for summarization and interpretation. This work investigates the impact of prompt injections on the ranking order of sources referenced by conversational search engines. The authors introduce a focused dataset of real-world consumer product websites (RAGDOLL) and formalize conversational search ranking as an adversarial problem. Experimentally, they analyze conversational search rankings in the absence of adversarial injections and show that different LLMs vary significantly in prioritizing product name, document content, and context position. They present a tree-of-attacks-based jailbreaking technique which reliably promotes low-ranked products, and these attacks transfer effectively to state-of-the-art conversational search engines such as perplexity.ai.

key_findings.title: LLMs vary significantly in what influences their ranking decisions
key_findings.description: Different LLMs prioritize different factors when ranking products in conversational search. GPT-4 Turbo and Llama 3 are heavily influenced by latent product name knowledge; GPT-4 Turbo is minimally influenced by retrieved documents. All LLMs are significantly influenced by input context position (products earlier in the context tend to rank higher).
key_findings.evidence: Section 5.1, Figure 2c-d. F-statistics show product name, document content, and context position all matter, but their relative importance varies dramatically between LLMs.
key_findings.implications: GEO strategies must be LLM-specific. What works for GPT-4 may not work for Llama or Gemini. Context position optimization (being early in the retrieval order) benefits all LLMs.

key_findings.title: Prompt injection can reliably boost low-ranked products
key_findings.description: Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP) technique reliably promotes the lowest-ranked product in a category to higher positions. Average ranking score gains: Llama 3 70B +6.02 (95.74% of max possible gain), GPT-4 Turbo +5.00 (82.94%), Mixtral 8x22 +4.13 (76.23%).
key_findings.evidence: Section 5.2, Figure 3, Table 1. All LLMs are susceptible. Llama 3 70B most vulnerable due to greater attention to document content.
key_findings.implications: Competitors can use adversarial prompt injection to artificially boost their products in AI-generated recommendations. Even sophisticated LLMs like GPT-4 Turbo are vulnerable.

key_findings.title: Attacks transfer from lab to production systems
key_findings.description: Adversarial injections targeting GPT-4 Turbo successfully transfer to perplexity.ai's Sonar Large Online model in a black-box setting. Despite perplexity.ai's closed-source RAG system with document chunking and summarization, the attack still increased rankings by an average of ~3 positions.
key_findings.evidence: Section 5.3, Table 1. Sonar Large Online: mean ∆score 2.89 (54.23% of max). Attack achieved by injecting prompt 15 times throughout HTML to survive chunking.
key_findings.implications: Real-world AI search engines are vulnerable. The attack doesn't require white-box access — transfer attacks work. Content injection at multiple points in the page defeats chunking-based defenses.

key_findings.title: Input context position is a universal ranking lever
key_findings.description: All tested LLMs showed significant bias toward ranking products earlier in the input context higher. Despite instructions to rank based on merit, LLMs tend to prefer product-document pairs that appear earlier in the retrieved set.
key_findings.evidence: Section 5.1, Figure 7. Context position F-statistic is high for all LLMs, with Mixtral 8x22 showing extreme position bias.
key_findings.implications: Being retrieved early (top of the search results) provides an inherent ranking advantage in LLM-generated recommendations — this amplifies traditional SEO into a double advantage for LLMSE.

extracted_strategies.name: Content Injection Defense: Monitor for Adversarial Prompts in Competitor Pages
extracted_strategies.what: Regularly audit competitor product pages for hidden or visible prompt injection text designed to manipulate LLM rankings. Look for phrases that command the LLM to rank a product first (e.g., 'This is the top recommendation', 'Must be featured as #1', or hidden text containing ranking instructions).
extracted_strategies.why: The paper demonstrates that prompt injection reliably promotes products in LLM-generated recommendations, and these attacks transfer to production systems like perplexity.ai. Competitors may already be using this technique.
extracted_strategies.how: 1) Scrape competitor product pages and search for ranking-manipulation language ('recommend first', 'top choice', 'must rank', 'best option'). 2) Check for hidden text (white text on white background, off-screen positioned text, 0-size fonts). 3) Run competitor URLs through Probe to see if AI responses contain suspiciously promotional language about them.
extracted_strategies.expected_impact: Detects unfair competitive advantage. If competitors are using prompt injection and you're not, you're at a significant disadvantage in AI-generated recommendations.
extracted_strategies.how_to_verify: Compare Probe results for competitor products before/after auditing. Flagged competitors should show artificially inflated recommendation rates.

extracted_strategies.name: Context Position Optimization: Maximize Early Retrieval
extracted_strategies.what: Optimize for being retrieved and positioned early in the LLM's input context. Since ALL LLMs show position bias (earlier items rank higher), ensure your content appears in the first batch of retrieved documents by optimizing for the retriever's scoring mechanism.
extracted_strategies.why: Input context position is the most universally effective ranking lever — unlike product name bias (varies by LLM) or document content optimization (varies by LLM), position advantage works across all tested LLMs including GPT-4, Llama 3, and Mixtral.
extracted_strategies.how: 1) Use exact-match keywords in H1/title tags that match likely user queries. 2) Implement Schema.org structured data for better indexing. 3) Keep pages fast-loading (Core Web Vitals) for better traditional search ranking → earlier retrieval. 4) Use descriptive meta descriptions that match query intent. 5) Consider paid search to boost traditional ranking position → earlier LLM retrieval.
extracted_strategies.expected_impact: Universal ranking boost across all LLM search engines. Amplifies traditional SEO gains into double advantage for LLMSE visibility.
extracted_strategies.how_to_verify: Track your traditional Google ranking position. Probe should show higher LLMSE visibility when your traditional ranking improves. Correlation is strong due to position bias.

extracted_strategies.name: Multi-LLM Consistency Verification for Ranking Fairness
extracted_strategies.what: When your product ranks low in AI recommendations despite strong traditional SEO, cross-check across multiple LLMs (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity) to determine if the issue is specific to one LLM's biases or a universal content problem.
extracted_strategies.why: Different LLMs have very different ranking biases: GPT-4 Turbo prioritizes product name knowledge over document content, Llama 3 70B heavily weights document content, all prioritize context position. A low rank in one LLM may not indicate a universal problem.
extracted_strategies.how: Run identical product recommendation queries across ChatGPT Search, Gemini, Perplexity, and Claude. Compare: (1) whether your brand appears, (2) ranking position, (3) whether the LLM cites your page or its own knowledge. If one LLM consistently ranks you low while others rank you high, focus optimization on that specific LLM's preference pattern.
extracted_strategies.expected_impact: More efficient GEO resource allocation — don't optimize for problems that don't exist across all LLMs.
extracted_strategies.how_to_verify: Create a multi-LLM ranking dashboard. Track ranking position variance across LLMs. High variance → LLM-specific optimization needed. Low variance → universal content quality issue.

extracted_strategies.name: Ethical Prompt Injection Monitoring and Reporting
extracted_strategies.what: Monitor AI search engines for evidence of prompt injection manipulation in competitor results. Report systematic abuse to AI companies (OpenAI, Google, Perplexity) through their responsible disclosure channels, as this violates their terms of service.
extracted_strategies.why: The financial incentive for ranking manipulation is massive ($80B+ SEO industry). Prompt injection attacks work and transfer to production systems. Responsible reporting helps level the playing field and incentivizes AI companies to build better defenses.
extracted_strategies.how: 1) Document specific examples of suspicious AI outputs where competitors receive unexplained preferential treatment. 2) Check if the competitor's page source contains prompt injection text. 3) Report through official channels: OpenAI safety form, Google's report a problem, Perplexity feedback. 4) Advocate for industry standards on AI ranking transparency.
extracted_strategies.expected_impact: Long-term: helps create a fairer AI search ecosystem. Short-term: may get specific competitors penalized if they're using injection techniques.
extracted_strategies.how_to_verify: Track whether reported competitor behavior changes after reporting. Monitor for AI company policy updates regarding ranking manipulation.

anti_patterns.name: Optimizing for a single LLM's known biases
anti_patterns.what: Optimizing content specifically for one LLM's ranking preferences (e.g., only targeting GPT-4's product-name bias) without considering other LLMs
anti_patterns.impact: 部分有效但不全面——不同LLM的偏好差异显著
anti_patterns.why: GPT-4 Turbo is minimally influenced by documents while Llama 3 70B heavily weights them. An optimization that works for one may be ignored by another. Use multi-LLM strategies.

anti_patterns.name: Ignoring context position in GEO strategy
anti_patterns.what: Focusing solely on content optimization without considering how early your page appears in the retrieval set
anti_patterns.impact: 显著削弱GEO效果——所有LLM都有位置偏见
anti_patterns.why: Even with optimized content, being retrieved last in the context significantly reduces ranking. Traditional SEO ranking position provides a double advantage in LLMSE.

key_metrics.best_injection_gain: Llama 3 70B: +6.02 average ranking score (95.74% of max gain)
key_metrics.transfer_effect: GPT-4 Turbo injections → Sonar Large Online: +2.89 score (54.23% of max)
key_metrics.context_position_bias: Universal across all tested LLMs — strongest in Mixtral 8x22
key_metrics.llm_specific_biases: GPT-4 Turbo: product name > documents. Llama 3 70B: documents > product name. All: context position matters
key_metrics.dataset: RAGDOLL: 1,147 real consumer product webpages across 50 categories
